page: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering two physical cores and two vCPUs at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data highlights exceptional single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core CPU test, Geekbench single-core score of 2806.0, and single-threaded gzip compression all ranking in the top tier. However, multi-threaded scaling presents a clear tradeoff; the multi-core Geekbench and stress-ng results fall into the average category, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang register in the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed, with cached small-block read bandwidth showing average throughput, while the Passmark memory score is positioned in the lower tier.

Given its high clock speed and strong single-threaded execution, the c8a.large offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. It is highly suitable for single-threaded batch processing, text processing, lightweight web serving, and basic database operations. It is less optimal for memory-intensive applications, multi-threaded software compilation, or parallelized rendering tasks due to its 4.0 GB memory limitation and lower multi-threaded scaling.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring two dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by high Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression benchmarks. However, its multi-threaded capabilities and memory scores are average to weak, reflecting a clear tradeoff for parallel workloads. This instance is highly efficient for single-threaded processing, text manipulation, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz and 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for targeted workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine delivering exceptional single-core performance and DDR5 memory for dedicated processing workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for single-threaded batch processing and light web serving
